title: Aureline external-effect lineage vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the external_effect_lineage_record and the external_effect_lineage_outcome_record frozen in docs/governance/runtime_authority_contract.md. A lineage packet captures, for one external-reaching or high-risk mutating effect, the frozen tuple (actor subject and class, approval / authority ticket refs, preview hash, target identity, side-effect class, outcome, and redaction posture) that support exports, emergency-action artifacts, mutation journal groups, evidence packets, and admin reconciliation flows all reuse instead of inventing parallel approval metadata. Raw secret bytes, raw URLs, raw delegated-token bodies, raw policy payloads, raw evidence bodies, and raw preview bodies never cross this boundary; lineage carries opaque refs and fingerprints only. Adding a new actor_class, side_effect_class, outcome_class, lineage_consumer_surface, approval_source_class, reconciliation_state, or redaction_class is additive-minor and requires an external_effect_lineage_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

external_effect_lineage_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this external-effect lineage export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / emergency-action records / evidence packets. Raw secrets, raw URLs, and raw delegated-token bodies never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

actor_class.description: Provider-actor class reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. unknown_actor_class is repair-only; surfaces MUST NOT collapse it with any other class and MUST NOT render a generic 'Connected' badge.

side_effect_class.description: Frozen side-effect class vocabulary. Matches the authority_ticket side_effect_class so lineage and ticket records project onto the same axis. Every lineage record names exactly one class.

approval_source_class.description: Frozen approval-source vocabulary. Every lineage record names exactly one approval source. no_approval_required is permitted only for side_effect_class = inspect_only; any other class with no_approval_required is a contract violation and MUST be rejected at validation time.

outcome_class.description: Frozen outcome vocabulary. failed_after_side_effect MUST carry a reconciliation_state; succeeded_with_downgrade and partial_success MUST carry a downgrade_reason.

downgrade_reason.description: Frozen downgrade-reason vocabulary for succeeded_with_downgrade and partial_success outcomes.

reconciliation_state.description: Frozen reconciliation-state vocabulary. Required on failed_after_side_effect outcomes; used by post-incident reconciliation hooks in the emergency-action model.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class shared with approval_ticket, authority_ticket, emergency_action_record, and record_state so every consumer reads the same redaction posture.

lineage_consumer_surface.description: Frozen consumer-surface vocabulary. Every lineage record declares at least one consumer surface. Surfaces outside this vocabulary MUST NOT import lineage packets; they request the support-bundle export path instead.

preview_hash_algorithm.description: Hash algorithm used to fingerprint the preview body. Raw preview bodies never cross the RPC boundary; the hash is the only artifact the lineage carries.

preview_hash_value.description: Hex-encoded hash digest of the preview body the user or admin approved against.

preview_record_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the durable preview record for consumer surfaces that need to resolve the full preview through their own access control.

preview_fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the exact preview the user or admin approved. Required for every side-effect class except inspect_only and automation_admission_only; absent for those two.

target_identity_ref.description: Opaque target-identity ref (ADR-0009 target-identity record id, connected_provider_record id, ADR-0007 credential-handle id, ADR-0009 execution-target id, or policy-subject id).

target_identity_fingerprint.description: Opaque fingerprint of target identity at effect time. Consumers that see a mismatch against the fingerprint on the authority ticket MUST NOT silently reconcile; they raise an emergency-action reconciliation.

authority_ticket_ref.description: Opaque authority_ticket_record id that admitted the effect. Required for approval_source_class = authority_ticket and for every approval_source_class that projects to a minted authority ticket (provider_approval_ticket, remembered_decision, policy_precomputed_decision, supervisor_control_path).

provider_approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque approval_ticket_record (provider plane) id when the effect crossed a provider mutation path; empty string otherwise.

remembered_decision_ref.description: Opaque ref to the remembered_decision_record that narrowed the admission, when applicable; empty string otherwise.

emergency_action_ref.description: Opaque ref to the emergency_action_record when approval_source_class is emergency_action_override; empty string otherwise.

ticket_lineage.description: Ordered list of predecessor authority ticket ids contributing to this effect; mirrors the authority_ticket ticket_lineage array.

approval_refs_packet.description: Frozen approval-refs packet. Consumer surfaces resolve approval context through these typed refs, not by re-reading the live ticket store.

issued_at.description: When the authority ticket admitting the effect was issued.

effect_started_at.description: When the runtime began executing the admitted effect.

effect_completed_at.description: When the effect reached a terminal outcome. Absent on pending outcomes.

rollback_completed_at.description: When a rollback completed, when outcome is rolled_back.

actor_subject.description: Acting identity the effect was executed as. Opaque subject id; raw secret material does not appear.

command_family_or_action_ref.description: Stable reference to the command family, provider action, credential projection, debug operation, or policy operation the lineage describes.

preview_fingerprint.description: Required (non-null) for every side-effect class except inspect_only and automation_admission_only.

workspace_or_workset_scope_ref.description: ADR-0009 workset / slice / workspace id the effect applies inside; empty string for policy-plane effects with no workspace scope.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id the effect ran inside.

sandbox_profile_or_capability_hash.description: Capability envelope the effect ran under.

consumer_surfaces.description: Surfaces that MAY consume this lineage packet. Consumer surfaces outside this list route through the support-bundle export path instead of importing the lineage directly.

allOf.description: preview_fingerprint MUST be non-null for every side-effect class except inspect_only and automation_admission_only.
no_approval_required is admissible only for inspect_only side-effect class.
authority_ticket_ref is required on approval_refs whenever the approval projects to a minted authority ticket.
emergency_action_ref is required on approval_refs when approval_source_class is emergency_action_override.

lineage_id.description: Opaque ref to the external_effect_lineage_record this outcome updates.

rollback_checkpoint_ref.description: Opaque ADR-0008 rollback-checkpoint ref the outcome restored, when applicable.

residual_effect_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence summarizing the residual effect when outcome_class is failed_after_side_effect or reconciliation_state is reconciled_with_residual_effect. Raw secret material and raw payload bytes MUST NOT appear.

superseding_lineage_ref.description: Opaque ref to a replacement lineage when outcome_class is superseded_by_emergency_action or when a re-run under new drift fingerprints produced a fresh lineage.

allOf.description: downgrade_reason is required when outcome_class is succeeded_with_downgrade or partial_success.
reconciliation_state is required when outcome_class is failed_after_side_effect.
superseding_lineage_ref is required when outcome_class is superseded_by_emergency_action.

external_effect_lineage_outcome_record.description: Outcome update for an external_effect_lineage_record. A lineage starts at outcome_class = pending and transitions to a terminal outcome exactly once; further changes emit a superseding lineage rather than editing the terminal outcome in place.
